Tests prototype inheritance and property ownership in JavaScript objects.

# Purpose
This code defines two constructor functions, [`Parent`](<#05-003Parent>) and [`Child`](<#05-003Child>), and demonstrates prototype inheritance in JavaScript. The [`Parent`](<#05-003Parent>) function initializes an object with a property `prop` set to 5. The [`Child`](<#05-003Child>) function initializes an object with a property `prop2` set to `false`. The prototype of [`Child`](<#05-003Child>) is set to [`Parent`](<#05-003Parent>), which means that instances of [`Child`](<#05-003Child>) will inherit properties from [`Parent`](<#05-003Parent>). An instance of [`Child`](<#05-003Child>) is created and stored in the variable `obj`. The `assert` statement checks that `obj` does not have its own property `prop`, confirming that `prop` is inherited from [`Parent`](<#05-003Parent>) through the prototype chain.
# Global Variables

---
### obj
- **Type**: ``Child``
- **Description**: An instance of the `Child` function constructor. The `Child` function sets the `prop2` property to `false` and inherits from the `Parent` function, which sets the `prop` property to `5`.
- **Use**: Used to create an object with properties defined in `Child` and inherited from `Parent`.
